title: Bacterial QS expands the Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris invasion of host tissue to trigger host-chlorophagy and maximize disease symptom
author: Samal, Biswajit
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: Using QS-responsive whole-cell bioreporters of  Xcc  and cabbage (  Brassica oleracea   )  as a model system and confocal microscopy, we show a detailed chronology of QS-facilitated  Xcc  colonization in the host mesophyll region. We report the QS-enabled bacterial localization of parenchymal chloroplast within heterogeneously invaded host mesophyll tissue, leading triggered leaf-chlorosis and systemic infection.
steps_raw: Using QS-responsive whole-cell bioreporters of  Xcc  and cabbage (  Brassica oleracea   )  as a model system and confocal microscopy, we show a detailed chronology of QS-facilitated  Xcc  colonization in the host mesophyll region. We report the QS-enabled bacterial localization of parenchymal chloroplast within heterogeneously invaded host mesophyll tissue, leading triggered leaf-chlorosis and systemic infection.
